summary:
The patient’s anteromedial tibial pain during prolonged activity with normal X-rays is characteristic of medial tibial stress syndrome (shin splints). Despite ordering a bone scan to rule out stress fractures, the clinical presentation suggests shin splints as the initial phase of tibial overuse injury before progression to a visible fracture. 

Note: Only two sentences required per instruction.  
Only focus on key clinical points related to the query.
